name: Element
description_short: This is not a consumer finance company—it's a federal government chemistry database (PubChem) displaying the periodic table of elements. The website URL is incorrectly configured.
description_long:
BACKGROUND: The provided website is PubChem's Periodic Table of Elements, an official United States government resource hosted by the National Institutes of Health (NIH) National Library of Medicine under the National Center for Biotechnology Information (NCBI). This is a chemistry and scientific reference tool, not a financial services provider.

WHAT THEY OFFER: The website displays an interactive periodic table of chemical elements with searchable properties including atomic mass, standard state, electron configuration, oxidation states, electronegativity, atomic radius, ionization energy, electron affinity, melting point, boiling point, density, and year discovered. Users can filter by chemical group/family and view elements as a table, list with properties, or interactive game format.

WHAT DISTINGUISHES THEM: As an official .gov website, PubChem provides secure (HTTPS-encrypted), authoritative scientific data. The periodic table includes all 118 known elements from hydrogen (H) through lawrencium (Lr) and synthetic elements. The interface offers multiple visualization formats and advanced search capabilities for chemistry professionals and students.

HONEST ASSESSMENT: This profile cannot be completed as a consumer finance company profile because the website contains zero financial services, lending products, credit tools, or consumer finance information. The categorization as "payday-alternatives" is factually incorrect. The website URL provided appears to be a data entry error, as PubChem is exclusively a scientific chemistry database with no connection to lending, credit, banking, or financial services of any kind. A valid consumer finance company profile requires an actual fintech or finance services provider website.
services: Interactive periodic table of elements (table view)
Element list with chemical properties display
Filterable view by chemical group/family (metals, nonmetals, halogens, noble gases, lanthanides, actinides, transition metals, etc.)
Melting and boiling point property data
Element density and atomic radius information
Year of element discovery historical data
